title: auth — compliance evidence
description: Auth is the enforcement layer for every authentication + session-management compliance obligation. NIST 800-63B (authentication), OWASP ASVS V3 (session management), OAuth 2.0 draft-ietf-oauth-security-topics (refresh rotation), GDPR + CCPA (session log retention + right-to-erasure cascade), SOC 2 (audit trail on every login/logout), COPPA (age-gate composition with the identity substrate). Every enforceable rule is codified as CODE + AUDIT.

controls.evidence: Password (memorized secret) + TOTP or WebAuthn (single-factor OTP or single-factor cryptographic device). Enforced by auth flow: LoginAction issues MFA challenge when identity.mfa_secret_encrypted IS NOT NULL; the second factor MUST complete before Sanctum PAT issues.
MFA challenges are single-use — consumed_at set atomically on successful verify. TOTP verifier accepts current window + ±30s drift; wider drift refused.
TOTP secret KMS-encrypted at rest (via identity substrate). WebAuthn attestation stored per FIDO2 spec; challenges live 5 minutes max.
Password_history retained as bcrypt/argon2id hashes only (identity substrate). No plaintext ever persisted.
Absolute + idle lifetimes per auth.session.*_ttl. Rotation on every refresh (auth.session.refresh_ttl_days). Deny-list on immediate revocation.
Sanctum PATs use 40-byte random secrets; refresh tokens use 40-byte random secrets. Both hashed SHA-256 at rest. Cryptographically random.
Only SHA-256 hashes persisted — secret plaintext returned once at issue-time only.
Password change fires PasswordChanged → revoke all sessions. MFA enrol/disable revokes sessions. User suspend/disable revokes sessions.
POST /auth/logout revokes PAT + paired refresh token in the same transaction.
auth.session.absolute_ttl_hours enforced as PAT.expires_at.

controls.principle: refresh token rotation with reuse detection
controls.evidence: Every refresh use consumes the old token (consumed_at set) + issues a new token in the same family_id. Re-presenting a consumed token is a REPLAY — fires RefreshTokenReuseDetected → invalidates the family + revokes every active PAT for the User. Canonical BCP defense.
Refresh tokens are polymorphically bound to a specific tokenable (User OR PlatformUser). Cross-User refresh replay is impossible.

Every token table has explicit retention (retention.json). Refresh tokens: 90 days. Password resets: 24h. Verifications: 30 days. MFA challenges: 1h. Cross-app grants: 24h. JWT deny list: natural expiry. Signing keys: 180 days after retirement.

Every auth-owned table cascades on identity_id delete. Full-Identity erasure (via identity/auth's DELETE /platform/identities/{id}) fires IdentityErased → auth listeners revoke all sessions + purge all tokens for the Identity.
IP addresses + user agents stored as SHA-256 hashes. Token secrets SHA-256 hashed at rest. JWT signing keys KMS-envelope-encrypted.
Rate limiting on every login endpoint. Progressive lockout via identity substrate. New-device notification. Refresh reuse detection. Every login/logout audit-logged.
RefreshTokenReuseDetected fires at critical severity + triggers ops paging. Suspicious-login-detection listener chain publishes an audit-log entry ops security ingests.
Session listing via GET /auth/sessions returns SessionSummaryData per active session — fulfills 'what sessions am I authenticated to' the DSAR requires.
Same as GDPR Art. 17 path. Cascades through identity_id.
Two guards enforced (sanctum, platform_admin). Guard boundary rejected at auth-service action layer. Every login audited.
Registration flow gates on CompliantAgePolicy + RegistrationPolicy. Cross-app SSO requires an existing target-Application User row (no auto-provisioning by default).
Rate limiting + progressive lockout + suspicious-login detection + strict CORS + refresh-reuse detection.

controls.principle: restrict physical + logical access to authenticator content
controls.evidence: Signing keys KMS-envelope-encrypted. TOTP secrets KMS-envelope-encrypted. Recovery codes bcrypt-hashed. Never plaintext at rest.
Auth-specific health probes (auth-jwt-signing-key-reachable, auth-refresh-purge-scheduler-alive, auth-jwt-rotation-current) + metrics feed operational monitoring.

RegisterAction composes CompliantAgePolicy (owned by this module) which resolves the effective floor from compliance.coppa.age_threshold. RegistrationPolicyInterface's default (GenericRegistrationPolicy) refuses; sports/athletes' AthleteRegistrationPolicy (Wave 7) routes to parent-consent workflow.
